mnemonics: CBT (Cardinal Bigger Than): Compare cardinals by finding an injection from the smaller set into the larger one, like a 'Can Be Traced' mapping.
keyPoints: The order relation |A| ≤ |B| is defined by the existence of an injective function from A to B.
The Schröder-Bernstein Theorem states that if |A| ≤ |B| and |B| ≤ |A|, then |A| = |B| (they are equipotent).
For any two sets A and B, at least one of |A| ≤ |B| or |B| ≤ |A| must hold, as stated by the Law of Trichotomy (equivalent to the Axiom of Choice).
commonMistakes: Assuming that because there is an injection from A to B, there must also be a surjection from B to A (this is only true in the finite case or if the sets are known to be equipotent).
Confusing the cardinality order (|A| ≤ |B|) with the standard numerical order and trying to apply arithmetic properties that don't hold for infinite cardinals.
